key_insight: The paper combines artificial neural networks with NSGA-II to analyze and optimize laser transmission welding performance for ABS, aiming to improve process quality through data-driven multi-objective tuning.
engineering_relevance: This is relevant for polymer joining and manufacturing because it offers a computational route to balance weld quality metrics and process efficiency without relying solely on trial-and-error experiments.
method: ANN models the relationship between welding parameters and performance outcomes
NSGA-II performs multi-objective optimization of the predicted responses
The integrated workflow supports parameter selection for improved weld characteristics
evidence_signal: From the abstract, the work is a journal article in the Arabian Journal for Science and Engineering indexed by OpenAlex; the supplied abstract does not report specific dataset size, metrics, or numerical validation results.
watch_item: The approach depends on the quality and representativeness of the training data, so the optimization may not generalize well outside the tested ABS welding conditions.
paper_title: Analysis and Enhancement of Performance Characteristics in Laser Transmission Welding of ABS via Machine Learning: Leveraging ANN and NSGA-II Integration
